title: Sandpile Cellular Automata (The Avalanche Simulator)
description: An Abelian sandpile model simulation with queue-based avalanche toppling engine, direct pixel-buffer rendering via putImageData, interactive click injection, and real-time criticality telemetry.
author: Girish Madarkar
features: Flat Uint16Array 2D grid with queue-based breadth-first topple engine processing up to 80,000 topples per frame
Direct Canvas Pixel Buffer manipulation via ctx.createImageData and Uint8ClampedArray injection — no per-cell fillRect calls
Abelian cascade physics: cells topple at threshold ≥4, distributing 4 grains to cardinal neighbors with exhaustive queue convergence
Interactive canvas click injection: 3000-grain cluster planted at cursor coordinates with instant avalanche resolution
Four preset seeding patterns: Central Peak Tower (10,000 grains), Cross-Grid Matrix, Random Cosmic Dusting, Corner Cascade
Live telemetry panel tracking total grains, per-frame topple count, generations elapsed, and criticality status (STABLE → SUPER-CRITICAL)
Adjustable grid resolution (50–250) and sand drop rate (0–100 grains/frame) with start/pause/step controls
